headline: Japan come from behind twice to hold the Netherlands 2-2 in their World Cup 2026 opener
summary: In Group F at AT&T Stadium, Dallas on June 14, 2026, Japan drew 2-2 with the Netherlands after twice fighting back from a goal down. Captain Virgil van Dijk headed the Oranje in front (51'); Keito Nakamura levelled six minutes later (57'). Crysencio Summerville restored the Dutch lead (64') but Daichi Kamada headed an 88th-minute equaliser to snatch a point for Japan. The Netherlands dominated the ball (60% possession) and chances (xG 0.79 to 0.54) but couldn't close it out — per match commentary the Netherlands had never previously failed to win a World Cup game in which they took the lead twice. The point caps a remarkable Japan run: they arrived on a five-game winning streak that included their first-ever victory over England (1-0, Mitoma 23', Wembley, 31 March 2026). Japan's resilience and Moriyasu's pressing system made the world's No.8 side blink; the Samurai Blue keep alive talk that this is their strongest-ever squad.
angles: Never-say-die Japan: down twice, level twice — Kamada's 88th-minute header steals a point
The streak rolls on: Japan beat England at Wembley, now hold the No.8 Netherlands — best Samurai Blue side ever?
Van Dijk's header wasn't enough: Netherlands had NEVER lost a WC lead twice and failed to win — until now
Dutch dominance, dropped points: 60% possession, more xG, two points gone
Kamada the late hero: the man who broke Dutch hearts in the 88th minute
Group F blown open: Japan announce themselves, Sweden & Tunisia still to come
